series_metadata.series_description: A twelve point suspension results when an individual accumulates 12 points on their driving record within a two-year period. At that time, the driver license is suspended in accordance with Section 4510.037 of the ORC. The individual is required to complete an eight hour remedial driving course, pay a thirty dollar reinstatement fee, file FRA insurance for a period of three to five Years and retake the complete driver license examination. All incoming correspondence relating to an individual suspension is stored in the image case folder.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: All paper documents are placed in the recycle bin 30 days after they are scanned. ; Microform: IT should scan file, if case has been closed for 7 years, delete records. All cases generated on/or after May 2001 are stored in imaging. Retain for 7 years from close date. ; Electronic: In accordance with section 4510.037 of the ORC the above suspensions are established and should be maintained under compliance., Paper: Recycle ; Microform: Delete ; Electronic: Shred
